_meta.purpose: Real resolution ground-truth for Epic 5 tests + source-state seed. Shows why the spec's raw auto-match thresholds are miscalibrated for this source.

venues.lesson: Clean match; KLMA external-id already stored from prior runs -> external-id ladder step hits first.
Exists but UNLINKED (no external id). Resolve should attach external id on match.

CORRECT venue, but name search = 20%. Two KLMA external ids already exist for ONE venue (variant drift). 0.95 threshold would CREATE A DUPLICATE. Only external-id/state hit + canonicalisation save this.
Correct, 69% < 0.95. Again two KLMA external ids for one venue. Same pattern as Cosey.
Clean. Note bndy uses MIXED id formats (UUID here vs Firestore-style elsewhere) - treat id as opaque string.

artists.lesson: Local act not in bndy -> CREATE. Region filter correctly avoided matching the famous national 'Tom Robinson'. Famous-name collisions are real; keep region as a guard for CREATE decisions but see Afterglow caveat.

100% match but artist location=NW England while KLMA defaultArtistLocation=Staffordshire. Tonight's gig is Crewe (NW). A HARD region=Staffordshire filter on artist resolve would MISS this and create a duplicate. KLMA spans Staffs+Cheshire/NW -> do not hard-filter artist matches to one region.
88% < 0.90 artistAutoMatch purely because of a SPACE ('Circa81' vs 'Circa 81'). Normalise (strip spaces/punct/case) BEFORE scoring or this becomes a duplicate.

The tool's own top 'recommendation' (Adam Morgan 82%) is WRONG. The real match (Adam George Forman, shares surname token) ranks lower at 61%. NEVER auto-accept top-1 by confidence; require shared significant token (surname) and route ambiguous to review.

thresholdFindings.evidence: Correct matches observed at name-confidence 100, 88, 69, 20. Top-1 recommendation can be a false positive (82%).
thresholdFindings.conclusion: Do NOT gate auto-match on the spec's raw name-confidence thresholds. Order the ladder: (1) source-state hit, (2) bndy external-id hit, (3) canonicalise+normalise then exact/near-exact, (4) Google place_id where present, (5) token-aware match. Reserve CREATE for genuine no-hit. Send the mushy middle to review, not auto-create.

recommendedResolverBehaviour: Treat bndy entity id as an opaque string (mixed UUID/Firestore formats coexist).
External-id + state lookup must come before name scoring (it is what actually prevents dupes here).
Canonicalise venue string (strip postcode/town/whitespace, normalise apostrophe) so the generated external-id slug is STABLE run-to-run.
On match with no stored external-id, ATTACH the source external-id (idempotent) instead of creating.
Do not hard-filter artist resolution to a single region; KLMA spans Staffordshire + Cheshire/NW.
Never auto-accept the search's top recommendation by confidence alone; require token agreement.
Pre-existing duplicate external-ids (Cosey x2, Nags Head x2) mean prior runs already drifted - the resolve endpoint should ideally collapse multiple source ids onto one bndy id.
